title: How to Create Custom Product Pages in GoHighLevel — Boost Conversions
description: Learn how to create custom product detail pages in GoHighLevel to boost conversions. Step-by-step guide for building on-brand PDPs that drive sales.
html_content:
If you're running a digital marketing agency or managing an ecommerce business, you know the frustration: generic product pages that look identical regardless of what you're selling. They don't tell your brand story. They don't highlight what makes your products unique. And worst of all, they don't convert as well as they could.

GoHighLevel's custom Product Detail Pages (PDPs) solve this problem. Instead of forcing every product into the same template, you can now build dedicated, on-brand pages that tell the right story for each product—and watch your conversion rates climb. Whether you're selling digital courses, physical products, or services, custom PDPs give you the control and flexibility top-performing businesses demand.

In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to create custom product pages in GoHighLevel, share best practices for maximum conversions, and show you why this feature is a game-changer for agencies. Ready to transform your ecommerce experience? Start your free 30-day trial and unlock the full power of custom PDPs today.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Are Custom Product Detail Pages in GoHighLevel?
  • Why Custom PDPs Boost Conversions
  • Step-by-Step: How to Create a Custom Product Page
  • Best Practices for Custom Product Pages
  • Routing Customers to Your Custom PDPs
  • Leveraging Custom PDPs for Brand Differentiation

What Are Custom Product Detail Pages in GoHighLevel?

Custom Product Detail Pages (PDPs) in GoHighLevel are purpose-built product pages you create directly within the platform's Site/Store Builder. Instead of relying on a default template that applies to all your products, you design unique pages mapped to specific products. This means your premium service offering can have a different layout, messaging, and visual design than your entry-level package—each optimized for its own audience and conversion goal.

Think of it this way: a default product page is like a one-size-fits-all shirt. A custom PDP is a tailored suit. It fits perfectly because it's designed specifically for that product.

This feature is built on GoHighLevel's familiar drag-and-drop builder, meaning you don't need coding skills. You get access to a rich toolkit of product elements—product lists, product details, pricing modules, and custom content blocks—that you arrange to match your brand and selling strategy.

Why Custom PDPs Boost Conversions

The numbers speak for themselves. When you give customers a product experience tailored to what they're actually buying, they're more likely to complete the purchase. Here's why custom PDPs work:

1. Storytelling Power
Each product has a narrative. Maybe one solves a pain point, another unlocks an opportunity. Custom PDPs let you tell that specific story instead of forcing generic language across all products.

2. Better Customer Experience
When layout, imagery, and copy align with the product itself, customers feel the difference. A course on productivity needs a different vibe than a luxury consultation package. Custom PDPs deliver that alignment.

3. Reduced Friction in the Buying Journey
Irrelevant information distracts buyers. With custom PDPs, every element on the page serves the conversion goal for that specific product. No noise, no confusion—just clarity.

4. Brand Differentiation
In competitive markets, a polished, on-brand product experience sets you apart. Agencies using custom PDPs consistently report higher perceived value and premium positioning.

Step-by-Step: How to Create a Custom Product Page

Step 1: Navigate to Your Store Builder
Log into GoHighLevel and go to your Site/Store Builder. Select the store where you want to add a custom product page. You'll see your product catalog and page management options.

Step 2: Create a New Custom Page
Instead of editing the default product template, create a brand new page. Name it something clear like "Premium Course PDP" or "VIP Consultation Page." This keeps your editor organized and makes it easy to manage multiple custom PDPs.

Step 3: Add Product Elements to Your Canvas
GoHighLevel provides dedicated product elements you drag onto your page: product details (name, description, price), product images, variants, reviews, and add-to-cart buttons. Place these strategically based on your design.

Step 4: Customize the Layout and Styling
Use the builder's styling options to match your brand. Adjust colors, fonts, spacing, and imagery. This is where your custom PDP becomes truly yours—not a generic template, but an extension of your brand identity.

Step 5: Add Supporting Content
Include sections that drive conversions: benefit highlights, social proof (testimonials, case studies), FAQs, comparison tables, or upsell recommendations. The builder lets you mix product elements with standard content blocks for maximum flexibility.

Step 6: Map the Page to Your Product
In your product settings, assign this custom page as the PDP for that specific product. GoHighLevel handles the routing—when someone clicks on that product, they land on this custom page instead of the default template.

Step 7: Test and Optimize
Preview the page on desktop and mobile. Test the add-to-cart flow, check that all product variants display correctly, and ensure the page loads quickly. Make adjustments as needed before going live.

💡 Pro Tip

Don't try to create the perfect custom PDP on day one. Start with your top-selling or highest-margin product. Build it, test it, measure the conversion lift, then use that winning template as a foundation for other PDPs. Iteration beats perfection in ecommerce.

Best Practices for Custom Product Pages

Lead with Benefits, Not Features
Your headline should answer the customer's unspoken question: "What's in it for me?" Don't just list what the product includes; explain the transformation or result the customer gets.

Use High-Quality Product Imagery
Invest in professional photos or lifestyle images that show your product in context. For digital products, consider screenshots, process visuals, or before-and-after comparisons. Images should reinforce your brand aesthetic.

Make the CTA Crystal Clear
Your add-to-cart button should be visually prominent and use action-oriented copy. "Add to Cart" works, but "Claim Your Access Now" or "Get Instant Delivery" can feel more urgent and aligned with your product.

Include Social Proof
Testimonials, reviews, and case studies dramatically increase confidence. If you have customer results or success stories, showcase them prominently on the custom PDP. Video testimonials convert even better than text.

Optimize for Mobile First
The majority of shopping happens on mobile. Make sure your custom PDP is responsive, readable on small screens, and doesn't require horizontal scrolling. Test thoroughly on iOS and Android devices.

Remove Unnecessary Navigation
One of the biggest conversion killers is too many exit routes. Consider hiding the main menu or reducing navigation options on product pages. You want customers focused on buying this product, not browsing elsewhere.

Routing Customers to Your Custom PDPs

Creating a custom PDP is only half the battle. You also need to route customers to it. Here's how:

From Product Listings
When you display a product list or catalog on your site, each product automatically links to its assigned PDP. GoHighLevel handles this routing seamlessly. If a product has a custom PDP assigned, clicking it goes to the custom page. If not, it uses the default template.

From Featured Sections
You can also highlight specific products on your homepage or category pages using featured product blocks. These respect the same routing logic—they link to the custom PDP if one exists.

Via Email and Marketing Campaigns
When sending promotional emails or running campaigns, link directly to the custom PDP URL. This ensures every traffic source lands on your optimized page.

From Your CRM and Automation
If you're using GoHighLevel's CRM and marketing automation, you can create customer journeys that link to specific custom PDPs based on their behavior, segment, or interests. This level of personalization drives serious conversion gains.

Leveraging Custom PDPs for Brand Differentiation

For digital marketing agencies, custom PDPs are a competitive weapon. When you present a client's products with a polished, strategic custom page, it signals professionalism and attention to detail. Here's how to use this strategically:

Develop Product-Specific Narratives
Your agency's voice should shift slightly based on the product. A B2B software solution needs different tone than a wellness course. Use custom PDPs to tell the right story for each product segment.

Showcase Case Studies and Results
Instead of generic product descriptions, build PDPs around specific customer results. Show before-and-after data, revenue increases, time saved—whatever metrics matter for that product. This builds credibility and justifies premium pricing.

Create Tiered Experiences
If you offer multiple pricing tiers, consider building slightly different custom PDPs for each. The entry-level PDP might emphasize quick wins and ease of use. The premium tier might focus on comprehensive support and advanced features.

Integrate with Your Brand Identity
Use custom PDPs to reinforce your brand's visual identity, voice, and values. Consistent, intentional design builds brand trust and makes your entire business feel more premium and professional.
